summary.oneLiner: Traditional coastal city with some of the best entry pricing on the Costa de Almería.
summary.shortDescription: Adra is a traditional fishing city with excellent beach access and the most affordable property prices on the coast. Perfect for value buyers seeking authentic Spanish coastal living.

pricing.buyerNote: One of the most affordable entry points on the coast with excellent value potential.

highlights.title: Playa de San Miguel
highlights.description: Beautiful Blue Flag beach with excellent facilities

highlights.title: Fishing Harbor
highlights.description: Active fishing port with daily fresh fish auctions

highlights.title: Cerro de la Peña
highlights.description: Scenic viewpoint overlooking the bay

editorial.vibe: Authentic Spanish coastal city with a working fishing heritage and friendly local community.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best entry pricing on the coast with excellent year-round livability and authentic Mediterranean charm.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: International buyers seeking expat communities or extensive tourist infrastructure.
editorial.diningNote: Excellent seafood restaurants around the harbor with daily fresh catches.
editorial.nearbyOutingNote: Easy day trips to the Sierra de Gádor mountains for hiking.

summary.oneLiner: Quiet beach village and one of the lower-cost coastal options.
summary.shortDescription: A peaceful coastal village offering authentic Spanish beach living at exceptional value. Perfect for those seeking tranquility away from tourist crowds.

pricing.buyerNote: One of the lowest-priced coastal properties in Almería province.

facts.climateSummary: Mediterranean climate with over 300 sunny days
facts.healthcareAccessNote: Nearest health center in El Ejido

highlights.title: Playa de Balanegra
highlights.description: Quiet, unspoiled beach perfect for peaceful days

highlights.title: Natural Dunes
highlights.description: Protected dune system behind the beach

editorial.vibe: Serene coastal village where time moves slowly and the beach is never crowded.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Exceptional value in a peaceful setting for those who prioritize beach access over amenities.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Those needing daily amenities, internet for remote work, or international community.

summary.oneLiner: Small, local seaside town with affordable apartment stock.
summary.shortDescription: A traditional Spanish seaside town with excellent beach access and affordable properties. Popular with Spanish families and offering authentic local culture.

pricing.buyerNote: Strong value for beach access, especially for apartments with sea views.

facts.climateSummary: Mediterranean with mild winters and hot summers
facts.healthcareAccessNote: Local health center with regular hours

highlights.title: Playa de Balerma
highlights.description: Long Blue Flag beach with excellent facilities

highlights.title: Seafront Promenade
highlights.description: Beautiful walking path along the beach

editorial.vibe: Genuine Spanish seaside town where locals holiday, with excellent beach access at reasonable prices.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best value for a town with proper infrastructure and lovely beaches, popular with Spanish families.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Those seeking an international expat community or English-speaking environment.

summary.oneLiner: Golf-and-marina lifestyle area with stronger second-home demand.
summary.shortDescription: A well-established resort with marina, golf course, and international character. Offers a good balance of amenities and Mediterranean charm.

pricing.buyerNote: Premium resort pricing with strong rental demand for holiday lets.

highlights.title: Marina
highlights.description: Full-service marina with boat facilities

highlights.title: Almerimar Golf
highlights.description: 18-hole golf course in resort setting

highlights.title: Beach Clubs
highlights.description: Multiple beach clubs along the coast

editorial.vibe: Established resort with international character, marina, and golf for those seeking managed leisure lifestyle.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best balance of resort amenities and authentic Mediterranean feel with strong rental potential.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Value buyers seeking cheaper entry points or those preferring quieter, more local settings.

summary.oneLiner: Long resort beaches, big local market, strong services and expat appeal.
summary.shortDescription: A major coastal town with extensive beachfront, excellent services, and a growing international community. Offers great value within a proper town setting.

pricing.buyerNote: Excellent value within a proper town with full services and beach access.

facts.healthcareAccessNote: Hospital de Roquetas with full emergency services

highlights.description: Established residential area with international feel

editorial.vibe: Major coastal town with excellent services, extensive beachfront, and growing international community.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best combination of full town services, beach access, and international character at mid-range prices.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Those seeking quiet village atmosphere or premium beachfront exclusivity.

summary.oneLiner: Marina-adjacent and popular for year-round living near the capital.
summary.shortDescription: A sophisticated beach district near Almería with marina, excellent restaurants, and proximity to the capital city. Popular with professionals working in Almería.
tradeoffs: Close to city but still touristy

pricing.buyerNote: Premium location near capital with excellent restaurants and marina lifestyle.

facts.healthcareAccessNote: Private clinic in Aguadulce, hospital in Almería

highlights.description: Easy access to historic capital for dining and culture

editorial.vibe: Sophisticated coastal district with marina, excellent dining, and easy access to Almería city.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Perfect for those wanting marina lifestyle with city proximity and excellent restaurants.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Value buyers or those seeking quieter, more traditional settings.

summary.oneLiner: Urban beach living, historic center, airport access, and the widest services.
summary.shortDescription: The provincial capital combines historic charm with beach access and full urban amenities. The only major city directly on the Mediterranean coast.

pricing.buyerNote: Capital city pricing with maximum services and airport proximity.

facts.climateSummary: Mediterranean - sunniest city in Europe with 320+ days

editorial.vibe: Provincial capital with Mediterranean soul, historic center, and full urban amenities including airport.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best for those needing full city services, airport access, and urban beach lifestyle combined.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Those seeking quiet village life or premium exclusivity away from urban bustle.

summary.oneLiner: Modern, lower-density beach zone near the airport and golf.
summary.shortDescription: A modern, purpose-built resort area with golf course, beach clubs, and excellent infrastructure. Popular with those seeking second homes near the airport.

pricing.buyerNote: Modern resort living near airport with golf and beach club access.

facts.healthcareAccessNote: Private clinic nearby, hospital in Almería

editorial.whyChooseIt: Best for golfers and those wanting modern resort living with easy airport access.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Those seeking traditional Spanish character or village atmosphere.

summary.oneLiner: The most sought-after village in the natural park, with supply constraints.
summary.shortDescription: The crown jewel of Cabo de Gata natural park. A picturesque village in a stunning protected landscape with excellent beaches and strong demand.

pricing.buyerNote: Highly constrained supply in protected park - strong appreciation potential.

facts.climateSummary: Mediterranean - warmest area on the coast

highlights.description: Iconic beach featured in Hollywood films

highlights.description: Main village beach with facilities

highlights.title: Cabo de Gata Natural Park
highlights.description: Europe's only desert biosphere reserve

editorial.vibe: Picturesque village in Spain's only desert natural park with stunning beaches and strong demand.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best for those seeking the unique combination of protected natural beauty and excellent beaches.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Value buyers or those needing full services and modern amenities.

sources: Parque Natural de Cabo de Gata

summary.oneLiner: Distinctive protected-coast setting with strong lifestyle demand.
summary.shortDescription: A charming fishing village on the less-visited eastern side of Cabo de Gata. Offers excellent beaches with more tranquility than San José.

pricing.buyerNote: More accessible than San José but still constrained by natural park designation.

highlights.description: Beautiful beach with volcanic black sand

highlights.description: Access to pristine natural park beaches

editorial.vibe: Charming fishing village on the wild side of Cabo de Gata with excellent beaches and more peace.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best for those seeking authentic village atmosphere and pristine beaches away from crowds.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Those needing daily amenities or fast internet for remote work.

summary.oneLiner: One of the priciest small beach villages on this coast.
summary.shortDescription: An exclusive small village with excellent beach and upscale dining. The premium location attracts high-net-worth buyers seeking privacy.

pricing.buyerNote: One of the most exclusive and expensive locations on the coast.

highlights.title: Playa de Agua Amarga
highlights.description: Beautiful cove beach with upscale feel

highlights.title: Exclusive Dining
highlights.description: Famous seafood restaurant overlooking the bay

editorial.vibe: Exclusive boutique village with one of the best beaches on the coast and refined atmosphere.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best for those seeking exclusivity, privacy, and one of the finest beaches in the region.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Value buyers or those seeking larger community with more services.

summary.oneLiner: More local-feeling town with access to striking eastern beaches.
summary.shortDescription: A traditional Spanish fishing town with excellent beaches and more authentic character than the resort areas. Offers good value with strong local culture.

pricing.buyerNote: Best value in Levante region with authentic Spanish coastal character.

editorial.vibe: Traditional Spanish coastal town with excellent beaches and authentic character.
editorial.whyChooseIt: Best for those seeking authentic Spanish coastal living with excellent beaches and good value.
editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Those seeking international community or resort-style amenities.

summary.oneLiner: Internationally popular resort market with consistently strong buyer demand.
summary.shortDescription: The most internationally known resort on the Costa de Almería. Offers excellent beach, established infrastructure, and strong rental demand.

pricing.buyerNote: Premium resort with strong rental demand and excellent infrastructure.

editorial.whoShouldAvoidIt: Value buyers seeking more affordable entry or those preferring quieter, less developed areas.

summary.oneLiner: Working port town with a practical year-round market.
summary.shortDescription: A genuine working fishing port with authentic Spanish character. Offers excellent seafood restaurants and a more local alternative to nearby resorts.

pricing.buyerNote: Best value in Levante area with authentic Spanish coastal town character.

summary.oneLiner: Resort-style stock with broad inventory and lifestyle appeal.
summary.shortDescription: A well-established resort with excellent beaches, good infrastructure, and strong rental demand. Popular with Northern European buyers.

summary.oneLiner: Quiet eastern option with marina feel and lower density.
summary.shortDescription: A peaceful coastal village on the eastern edge with marina and beach. Offers good value with a quieter atmosphere than the busier resorts.

summary.oneLiner: Far-east lifestyle market close to the Murcia border.
summary.shortDescription: The easternmost resort on the Almería coast, close to the Murcia border. Offers excellent beaches and a more laid-back atmosphere than central areas.

pricing.buyerNote: Best value in far-east with access to Murcia amenities nearby.
